---
title: "HIKET — Data Preparation"
subtitle: "Methods and pipeline documentation"
author: "Lorenzo Menichetti"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %Y')`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
    latex_engine: xelatex
geometry: margin=2.5cm
fontsize: 11pt
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Overview

This document describes the data preparation pipeline for the HIKET Bayesian
SOC model calibration study. The pipeline is implemented in `Data_work.R` and
produces three output files in `Data/model_inputs/`:

| File | Content | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| `input_raw_monthly.csv` | Litter inputs + climate | plot × year × month |
| `climate_monthly.csv` | Climate normals | plot × year × month |
| `site_raw.csv` | Site attributes + `calib_ready` flag | plot |

The models calibrated are Yasso07, Yasso15, and Yasso20 (RothC forthcoming).
All use the same calibration plot set, defined by the `calib_ready` filter
described in Section 5.

---

# Data Sources

## SOC Observations

The SOC data (`Data/SOC/soilC1985_2006.csv`) compiles measurements from two
national soil C campaigns conducted on the Finnish NFI permanent plot network
(3,009 plots established in 1985--86 as part of VMI, the Finnish National
Forest Inventory):

**Campaign 1 --- VMI8, 1985--86.**
The first Finnish national forest inventory permanent plot network, including
soil C measurements. Mineral layer protocol: 0--5 cm and 5--20 cm measured
separately, plus 20--40 cm. 414 plots with soil data.

**Campaign 2 --- Biosoil, 2006.**
European-wide Level I soil monitoring campaign coordinated by ICP Forests /
EU. The Finnish contribution measured soil properties including SOC stocks at
the same NFI permanent plots (linked via `bio_soil_id` in the NFI plot
records). Mineral layer protocol: 0--10 cm and 10--20 cm (different
aggregation from 1985), plus 20--40 cm. Same 414 plots as the 1985 campaign.

**Campaign 3 --- Komeetta (pending).**
A new Finnish national soil C campaign on the same permanent plot network.
Data not yet received; when available, it will extend the calibration to a
third observation year and increase the power of the temporal
model--observation comparison.

**File structure:** `soilC1985_2006.csv` contains 414 unique plots × 2
years × multiple layers. Each plot appears in both years (perfectly
symmetric: 1,695 rows per year). The layer name difference between campaigns
(0--5/5--20 cm in 1985 vs 0--10/10--20 cm in 2006) is handled in
`Data_work.R` by accepting both aggregation conventions.

- **Layers accepted:** organic (OFH), 0--5 cm, 5--20 cm, 0--10 cm,
  10--20 cm, 20--40 cm.
- **Coverage after matching with litter data:** 412 plots.

Observations are placed in June of the respective measurement year in the
monthly input table, consistent with the summer measurement period of the
Finnish NFI.

## Litter Inputs

Annual tree litter carbon inputs from Tupek et al. (2026), Zenodo DOI
[10.5281/zenodo.19736499](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19736499).

The dataset integrates NFI permanent plot data from 2805 Finnish sites
(inventory rounds 1985, 1990, 1995, 2023) with satellite-derived basal area
observations (2005–2023) to produce continuous annual time series 1985–2023.
Litter inputs from living biomass turnover and harvest residues are summed to
total litter, then distributed across three size classes and four AWEN
chemical fractions.

**Size classes:**

| Code | Description |
|---|---|
| `nwl` | Non-woody litter (foliage + fine roots) |
| `fwl` | Fine woody litter (branches + coarse roots + stem bark) |
| `cwl` | Coarse woody litter (stumps) |

**AWEN fractions:** acid-soluble (a), water-soluble (w), ethanol-soluble (e),
non-soluble/lignin (n). Fraction proportions are species- and
component-specific. Carbon content assumed 50% of dry mass throughout
(i.e., Cha = 0.5 × litter dry mass × AWEN fraction).

- **Files used:**
  - `Data/LitterData/tree_litter_per_site_year_awen_29.04.26.csv` — aggregated
    across components, resolved by site × year × species × size × AWEN
  - `Data/LitterData/tree_litter_per_site_year_by_component_29.04.26.csv` —
    component-level detail (used for diagnostics)
- **Coverage after plot-key matching:** 2794 plots; 412 have both litter and
  SOC data.
- **Units:** t C ha⁻¹ yr⁻¹.

Plot-key linkage between the three identifier systems (MUSTIKKA, VANHA, BIOSOIL)
is performed via `Data/soil_litter_site_key.csv`.

## Climate

Daily gridded meteorological data for all Finnish NFI permanent plots.

- **File:** `Data/Climate/nfi_plot_weather_data_1961_2025.nc`
- **Variables extracted:** mean, min, and max air temperature (°C);
  precipitation (mm d⁻¹); global radiation (W m⁻²).
- **Period:** 1961–2025.
- **Aggregation:** daily values aggregated to monthly means (temperature) and
  sums (precipitation, PET).

Potential evapotranspiration (PET) is computed via the Hargreaves–Samani
equation:

$$\text{PET} = 0.0023 \times \frac{R_g}{1000} \times \sqrt{T_{\max} - T_{\min}} \times (T_{\text{avg}} + 17.8)$$

where $R_g$ is global radiation (W m⁻² d⁻¹) and temperatures are in °C.

## Soil Properties — Biosoil

2006 European soil monitoring campaign, Finnish subset.

- **Files:** `../../Datasets/BioSoil_maaperäaineisto_2006/soil_data_main.csv`
  and `plot_index.csv`.
- **Mineral topsoil:** layers M01 (0–10 cm) and M12 (10–20 cm) averaged.
  Variables include texture (clay, silt, sand), bulk density (measured and
  estimated), pH, exchangeable cations, organic C, total N, organic matter.
- **Organic layer (OFH):** depth and weight.
- **Cajander fertility class (KA):** 1–4 for mineral sites; 11–13 for
  peatland types (excluded from calibration, see Section 5).
- **Biogeographic zones:** SYKE administrative zones (`kasvyo_syke`, 5 levels
  south to north) and Ahti (1968) floristic zones (`kasvyo_ahti`).
- **Coverage:** 99.8% of SOC plots matched to Biosoil records.

Derived soil chemistry variables computed in the pipeline:

- `CN_ratio` = OrganicCarbon / TotalNitrogen
- `CEC` = sum of exchangeable cations + free H acidity
- `base_saturation` = (Ca + Mg + K + Na) / CEC

## GTK Soil Classification

Geological Survey of Finland (GTK) 1:1M soil map, queried via ArcGIS REST
API (MapServer layer 3, endpoint:
`gtkdata.gtk.fi/.../Hasu/maapera/MapServer/3`). Soil codes include mineral
classes (Mr, HM, Sa, …) and non-mineral classes (Tu = peat, Ve = water,
Ka = bedrock).

Used for: (1) descriptive soil type field in `site_raw`; (2) per-class pooled
λ estimation in the SOC depth extrapolation. **Not used as a calibration
filter** (see Section 5.3).

## NFI Stand Attributes — Kuvio

Stand-level attributes from the Finnish NFI permanent plot records
(`PysyvätKoealat`), three measurement waves.

- **Files:** `Data/PysyvätKoealat/{1985,1990,1995}/kuvio.csv`
- **Key variables extracted:** stand age, basal area, mean height, development
  class, site fertility type (`kasvup_tyyppi`, 8 classes), main site type
  (`alaryhma`), drainage status (`ojitustilanne`), cutting events
  (`hakk_laatu`), stand treatments (`mets_h_toim`), soil preparation
  (`aiempi_toim`), temperature sum (`lamposumma`), elevation.
- **Temporal coverage of management indicators:** 1985 wave covers cuttings
  ≤10 years back and soil preparation ≤30 years back; 1990 records new events
  since 1985; 1995 covers 1990–1995. Combined: approximately 1955–1995.
- **Note on `kasvup_tyyppi`:** this 8-class NFI site fertility system (1 =
  herb-rich *lehdot* → 8 = fell/subalpine) is more informative than the
  Cajander class KA (4 mineral classes) for characterising the climate–
  productivity relationship across the calibration set.

---

# SOC Depth Extrapolation to 1 m

Yasso is parametrised for a 1 m soil profile. Measured data cover OFH +
mineral 0–40 cm only. The 40–100 cm tail is estimated by an exponential depth
model:

$$C(z) = C_0 \cdot e^{-\lambda z}$$

fitted in two stages to balance shape stability with per-profile amplitude
flexibility.

**Stage 1 — Pooled λ per GTK soil class.**
All profiles within a soil class are pooled. $C_0$ is concentrated out
analytically at each candidate λ via no-intercept OLS (closed-form solution),
reducing the optimisation to a 1-D search over λ alone. A global fallback λ
(all classes pooled, λ ≈ 0.026 cm⁻¹) is computed for codes with too few
profiles.

**Stage 2 — Per-profile $C_0$ rescaling.**
Holding the class λ fixed, $C_0$ is solved analytically per profile via
no-intercept OLS against the observed layer stocks. This anchors the depth
shape to the class while allowing each profile its own amplitude.

**Layer integral form** (fitted directly to observed layer stocks, no midpoint
approximation):

$$\text{Stock}(z_1, z_2) = \frac{C_0}{\lambda} \left(e^{-\lambda z_1} - e^{-\lambda z_2}\right)$$

**Total 1 m SOC** = OFH stock (measured, added directly) + mineral 0–40 cm
(measured, summed) + mineral 40–100 cm (extrapolated). The raw 0–40 cm + OFH
sum is retained as `soc_obs_tCha_sum` for diagnostic purposes. Of 824
plot-year combinations attempted, 822 were successfully extrapolated (2 lacked
sufficient mineral layer data and fall back to the raw sum).

---

# Organic Layer Quality Filters

Two organic layer flags are computed from the SOC data and propagate into the
`calib_ready` filter.

- **`organic_missing`:** OFH layer never recorded in any measurement year.
  Indicates a structural data gap; the 1 m extrapolation cannot be performed
  without an OFH stock. Affects 12 plots.
- **`organic_zero`:** OFH recorded but C_kgha = 0 in at least one year.
  Likely a measurement issue rather than genuine absence; excluded out of
  caution. Verified non-overlapping with `organic_missing`. Affects 28 plots.

---

# Calibration-Ready Plot Filtering

The `calib_ready` flag in `site_raw` defines the calibration set. A plot is
included if and only if all of the following hold:

| # | Criterion | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `has_climate` | Plot present in climate netCDF with non-NA temperature |
| 2 | `has_soc` | At least one SOC observation in `SOC_agg` |
| 3 | `!peatland` | Cajander KA ∉ {11, 12, 13}; peatland C dynamics not represented by Yasso |
| 4 | `!organic_missing` | OFH never recorded; extrapolation impossible |
| 5 | `!organic_zero` | OFH recorded as zero in any year; excluded as data quality issue |
| 6 | `!high_mrt` | Implied MRT ≤ 100 years (see below) |

**Implied MRT filter (criterion 6).** Implied MRT = mean(1 m SOC) /
mean(annual litter input). Plots with MRT > 100 years have litter inputs so
low that the model predicts near-zero SOC at any parameter value, causing a
−∞ likelihood at the pre-MCMC sanity check. The 100-year threshold corresponds
to a clear gap in the MRT distribution. This filter also catches four plots
with zero litter after AWEN-compound mapping (MRT = ∞) that bypass criterion
5, which operates on raw litter inputs before compound-fraction assignment.
In total, 14 plots are excluded (4 with MRT = ∞, 10 with MRT 100–489 years);
several overlap with GTK peat/water classes, consistent with peat-influenced
carbon dynamics unsupported by Yasso's mineral-soil parameterisation.

**GTK soil class — reviewed, not used as a filter.** Tu (peat), Ve (water),
and Ka (bedrock) classes together constitute ~26% of plots passing criteria
1–6. Inspection showed: (a) SOC distributions for these classes are
statistically indistinguishable from mineral plots (Tu median 59, Ve 59, Ka
70, mineral 62 Mg C ha⁻¹); (b) Cajander vegetation surveys classified 53 of
96 such plots as KA = 1 (rich mineral upland forest), with none classified as
peatland (KA 11–13). The disagreement is consistent with the GTK map's coarse
resolution (1:1,000,000). GTK class is retained in `site_raw` for descriptive
use and for per-class λ estimation in the depth extrapolation.

**Final calibration set: 351 plots** (365 before the MRT filter; 350–351
after matching with climate and SOC records).

---

# Derived Site Covariates (site_raw)

The following covariates are computed and stored in `site_raw` for use in the
residual random-forest analysis.

## Climate Normals and Variability

Computed from long-run monthly normals (all available years in the netCDF):

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `mean_temp` | Annual mean air temperature (°C) |
| `mean_precip_annual` | Annual precipitation sum (mm yr⁻¹) |
| `PET_annual` | Annual Hargreaves–Samani PET sum |
| `coldest_month_T`, `warmest_month_T` | Monthly temperature extremes |
| `T_seasonality` | SD of monthly temperature normals |
| `P_seasonality` | CV of monthly precipitation normals |
| `GDD5` | Growing degree days base 5 °C (weighted by calendar-month length) |
| `aridity_index` | mean_precip_annual / PET_annual |
| `koppen_class` | Köppen–Geiger class (Beck et al. 2018, simplified; Dfb or Dfc for Finland) |
| `temp_zone` | Tercile binning of mean_temp into cold / intermediate / warm |

## Litter Quality

Computed from cumulative litter totals across the full time series:

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `litter_{A,W,E,N}_frac` | AWEN compound fractions of total plot litter |
| `woody_share` | (fwl + cwl) / (nwl + fwl + cwl) |
| `conifer_share` | sp_frac_1 (Scots pine) + sp_frac_2 (Norway spruce) |

All three size classes (nwl, fwl, cwl) are sourced directly from the Tupek
et al. dataset.

## Soil Chemistry

Derived from Biosoil mineral topsoil (M01 + M12 layers, averaged):

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `CN_ratio` | OrganicCarbon / TotalNitrogen (NA where N = 0) |
| `CEC` | Sum of exchangeable cations + free H acidity |
| `base_saturation` | (Ca + Mg + K + Na) / CEC |

For RF covariate selection: prefer `EstimatedBulkDensity` over
`MeanBulkDensity` (the latter has 86% NA in the calibration set). Avoid using
`OrganicCarbon` and `OrganicMatter` from Biosoil as RF covariates — they are
highly collinear with the SOC observations and would trivially recover the SOC
signal rather than model residual structure.

---

# Output Files

## input_raw_monthly.csv

Monthly time series, one row per (plot_id, year, month). Coverage: 405 plots
with full climate data, 1985–2023, 12 months/year = 189,540 rows.

Litter inputs in t C ha⁻¹ month⁻¹ (annual values divided by 12, i.e. assuming
uniform within-year distribution). SOC observations placed in June of the
observation year. Plots without full climate coverage are excluded; SOC
observations for excluded plots are not carried forward.

## climate_monthly.csv

Standalone monthly climate per plot, deduplicated. Same plot coverage as
`input_raw_monthly.csv`.

## site_raw.csv

Comprehensive per-plot attributes. 419 total plots; 351 with `calib_ready =
TRUE`. Columns cover all covariates listed in Section 6 plus all raw Biosoil,
GTK, and kuvio variables, exclusion flags (`peatland`, `organic_missing`,
`organic_zero`, `high_mrt`), and the `calib_ready` flag.

---

# Automated Sanity Checks

The script ends with 25 automated checks. Key checks relevant to the
calibration:

- No duplicate plot–year–month rows
- No NA in climate fields
- All plots have the same month count (468 rows = 39 years × 12 months)
- No negative litter values
- SOC observations placed only in June
- SOC count consistent between `input_raw_monthly` and `SOC_agg`
- Litter magnitude median within boreal range (1.5–4.5 t C ha⁻¹ yr⁻¹;
  observed median 2.56 after CWL correction)
- SOC plausibility for calib-ready plots (5–400 t C ha⁻¹)
- 1 m extrapolated median ≥ raw sum median
- No peatland, organic-excluded, or high-MRT plots in `calib_ready`
- All derived covariates present in `site_raw`
- AWEN fractions sum to 1 per plot

---

# Software and Reproducibility

**Language:** R (≥ 4.3).  
**Key packages:** `sf`, `ncdf4`, `httr`, `jsonlite`, `rnaturalearth`, `dplyr`,
`ggplot2`.  
**HPC:** Production runs on CSC Puhti (Finland); development on macOS.

To reproduce from scratch, run `Data_work.R` from the project root directory.
The GTK soil type extraction (section 1.3) requires internet access to the
GTK ArcGIS REST API; all other steps are self-contained given the data files.
After running, sync outputs to Puhti and recompile the Fortran shared libraries
before submitting calibration jobs (see `Calibration_real_data/run_hiket_pipeline.R`
for the full workflow).
